description: National Championship Standard performance thresholds. Metrics below these values are flagged as 'Doctrine Deviations.' Thresholds derived from Texas championship-season averages and modern CWS-qualifier benchmarks.

standards.description: Team batting average. Championship-caliber Texas teams average .285+ at the plate.
Team on-base plus slugging. An OPS of .800+ reflects the offensive identity of a championship contender.
Team ERA. Championship rotations hold opponents to 3.50 ERA or better. Lower is better.
Team WHIP. Elite pitching staffs surrender fewer than 1.25 baserunners per inning. Lower is better.
Team fielding percentage. Championship caliber requires .970+ to minimize unearned runs.
Pitching strikeouts per 9 innings. Modern championship staffs produce 9+ K/9.
Offensive runs per game. Championship offenses score 6.5+ runs per game on average.
